title: PeerMesh Module Lifecycle Hooks
description: Schema for module lifecycle hook definitions

install.description: Called when module is added to the system. One-time setup: create directories, initialize state, run migrations.

start.description: Called when module is activated. Start services, connect to resources, begin processing.

stop.description: Called when module is deactivated. Graceful shutdown: close connections, flush buffers, save state.

uninstall.description: Called when module is removed. Cleanup: remove directories, delete state, drop database schemas.

health.description: Called periodically or on-demand to check module health. Returns health status.

upgrade.description: Called when module version changes. Run version-specific migrations.

validate.description: Called before install to validate configuration and dependencies.

security-provision.description: Called after identity credential issuance and encryption key provisioning. Module can perform security-specific setup.

security-deprovision.description: Called before identity revocation and key eviction. Module should clean up security-related state.

security-rotate.description: Called when credentials or keys are rotated. Module should update to use new credentials/keys.

security-lock.description: Called when session locks (if module has security.session.lockBehavior set). Module should respond to lock event.

oneOf.description: Path to executable script relative to module root
No hook defined for this lifecycle event

script.description: Path to executable script relative to module root

retries.description: Number of retry attempts on failure

environment.description: Additional environment variables for this hook
